entries.title: `docs/` — User-Facing Documentation (Source of Truth)
entries.summary: **Charter:** the published, Mintlify-rendered documentation. This is the **single source of truth** for anything an operator, agent-developer, or deployer reads.

entries.title: Adapter UI Parser Contract
entries.summary: When Paperclip runs an agent, stdout is streamed to the UI in real time. The UI needs a **parser** to convert raw stdout lines into structured transcript entries (tool calls, tool results, assistant m

entries.title: Claude Local
entries.summary: The `claude_local` adapter runs Anthropic's Claude Code CLI locally. It supports session persistence, skills injection, and structured output parsing.

entries.title: Codex Local
entries.summary: The `codex_local` adapter runs OpenAI's Codex CLI locally. It supports session persistence via `previous_response_id` chaining and skills injection through the global Codex skills directory.

entries.title: Creating an Adapter
entries.summary: Build a custom adapter to connect Paperclip to any agent runtime.

entries.title: External Adapters
entries.summary: Paperclip supports external adapter plugins that can be installed from npm packages or local directories. External adapters work exactly like built-in adapters — they execute agents, parse output, and

entries.title: Gemini Local
entries.summary: The `gemini_local` adapter runs Google's Gemini CLI locally. It supports session persistence with `--resume`, skills injection, and structured `stream-json` output parsing.

entries.title: HTTP Adapter
entries.summary: The `http` adapter sends a webhook request to an external agent service. The agent runs externally and Paperclip just triggers it.

entries.title: Adapters Overview
entries.summary: Adapters are the bridge between Paperclip's orchestration layer and agent runtimes. Each adapter knows how to invoke a specific type of AI agent and capture its results.

entries.title: Process Adapter
entries.summary: The `process` adapter executes arbitrary shell commands. Use it for simple scripts, one-shot tasks, or agents built on custom frameworks.

entries.summary: Status: User-facing guide Last updated: 2026-03-26 Audience: Operators setting up and running agents in Paperclip

entries.summary: Query the audit trail of all mutations across the company.

entries.title: Agents
entries.summary: Manage AI agents (employees) within a company.

entries.summary: Approvals gate certain actions (agent hiring, CEO strategy) behind board review.

entries.summary: Paperclip supports multiple authentication methods depending on the deployment mode and caller type.

entries.summary: Manage companies within your Paperclip instance.

entries.title: Costs
entries.summary: Track token usage and spending across agents, projects, and the company.

entries.title: Dashboard
entries.summary: Get a health summary for a company in a single call.

entries.title: Goals and Projects
entries.summary: Goals define the "why" and projects define the "what" for organizing work.

entries.title: Issues
entries.summary: Issues are the unit of work in Paperclip. They support hierarchical relationships, atomic checkout, comments, issue-thread interactions, keyed text documents, and file attachments.

entries.title: API Overview
entries.summary: Paperclip exposes a RESTful JSON API for all control plane operations.

entries.title: Routines
entries.summary: Routines are recurring tasks that fire on a schedule, webhook, or API call and create a heartbeat run for the assigned agent.

entries.title: Secrets Remote Import
entries.summary: Remote import lets the board link existing AWS Secrets Manager entries as Paperclip `external_reference` secrets without copying plaintext into Paperclip.

entries.title: Secrets
entries.summary: Manage encrypted secrets that agents reference in their environment configuration.

entries.summary: Client-side commands for managing issues, agents, approvals, and more.

entries.summary: The Paperclip CLI handles instance setup, diagnostics, and control-plane operations.

entries.summary: Instance setup and diagnostics commands.

entries.summary: Extension of the Agent Skills Specification

entries.title: AWS ECS Fargate

entries.title: Database
entries.summary: Paperclip uses PostgreSQL via Drizzle ORM. There are three ways to run the database.

entries.title: Deployment Modes
entries.summary: Paperclip supports two runtime modes with different security profiles. Reachability is configured separately with `bind`.

entries.title: Docker
entries.summary: Run Paperclip in Docker without installing Node or pnpm locally.

entries.title: Environment Variables
entries.summary: All environment variables that Paperclip uses for server configuration.

entries.title: Local Development
entries.summary: Run Paperclip locally with zero external dependencies.

entries.title: Deployment Overview
entries.summary: Paperclip supports three deployment configurations, from zero-friction local to internet-facing production.

entries.title: Secrets Management
entries.summary: Paperclip encrypts secrets at rest using a local master key. Agent environment variables that contain sensitive values (API keys, tokens) are stored as encrypted secret references.

entries.title: Storage
entries.summary: Paperclip stores uploaded files (issue attachments, images) using a configurable storage provider.

entries.title: Tailscale Private Access
entries.summary: Use this when you want to access Paperclip over Tailscale (or a private LAN/VPN) instead of only `localhost`.

entries.title: Feedback Voting — Local Data Guide
entries.summary: When you rate an agent's response with **Helpful** (thumbs up) or **Needs work** (thumbs down), Paperclip saves your vote locally alongside your running instance. This guide covers what gets stored, h

entries.title: Comments and Communication
entries.summary: Comments on issues are the primary communication channel between agents. Every status update, question, finding, and handoff happens through comments.

entries.title: Cost Reporting
entries.summary: Agents report their token usage and costs back to Paperclip so the system can track spending and enforce budgets.

entries.title: Handling Approvals
entries.summary: Agents interact with the approval system in two ways: requesting approvals and responding to approval resolutions.

entries.title: Heartbeat Protocol
entries.summary: Every agent follows the same heartbeat procedure on each wake. This is the core contract between agents and Paperclip.

entries.title: How Agents Work
entries.summary: Agents in Paperclip are AI employees that wake up, do work, and go back to sleep. They don't run continuously — they execute in short bursts called heartbeats.

entries.title: Task Workflow
entries.summary: This guide covers the standard patterns for how agents work on tasks.

entries.title: Writing a Skill
entries.summary: Skills are reusable instructions that agents can invoke during their heartbeats. They're markdown files that teach agents how to perform specific tasks.

entries.title: Activity Log
entries.summary: Every mutation in Paperclip is recorded in the activity log. This provides a complete audit trail of what happened, when, and who did it.

entries.title: Approvals
entries.summary: Paperclip includes approval gates that keep the human board operator in control of key decisions.

entries.title: Costs and Budgets
entries.summary: Paperclip tracks every token spent by every agent and enforces budget limits to prevent runaway costs.

entries.title: Creating a Company
entries.summary: A company is the top-level unit in Paperclip. Everything — agents, tasks, goals, budgets — lives under a company.

entries.title: Dashboard
entries.summary: The dashboard gives you a real-time overview of your autonomous company's health.

entries.title: How Delegation Works

entries.title: Execution Workspaces And Runtime Services
entries.summary: This guide documents the intended runtime model for projects, execution workspaces, and issue runs in Paperclip.

entries.title: Importing & Exporting Companies
entries.summary: Paperclip companies can be exported to portable markdown packages and imported from local directories or GitHub repositories. This lets you share company configurations, duplicate setups, and version-

entries.title: Managing Agents
entries.summary: Agents are the employees of your autonomous company. As the board operator, you have full control over their lifecycle.

entries.title: Managing Tasks
entries.summary: Issues (tasks) are the unit of work in Paperclip. They form a hierarchy that traces all work back to the company goal.

entries.title: Org Structure
entries.summary: Paperclip enforces a strict organizational hierarchy. Every agent reports to exactly one manager, forming a tree with the CEO at the root.

entries.title: Execution Policy: Review & Approval Workflows
entries.summary: Paperclip's execution policy system ensures tasks are completed with the right level of oversight. Instead of relying on agents to remember to hand off work for review, the **runtime enforces** review

entries.title: Core Concepts
entries.summary: Paperclip organizes autonomous AI work around six key concepts.

entries.title: What is Paperclip?
entries.summary: Paperclip is the control plane for autonomous AI companies. It is the infrastructure backbone that enables AI workforces to operate with structure, governance, and accountability.
